title: Notopappus pectinatus
intro: Notopappus pectinatus, also known as the coastal bush marigold, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its bright yellow and orange daisy-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as colds, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Notopappus pectinatus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural expectorant.
taxonomy: Notopappus pectinatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Mutisieae and is native to South America. The classification of Notopappus pectinatus is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, tribe Mutisieae, genus Notopappus, species N. pectinatus. This plant is known for its unique characteristics and is an important part of the local ecosystem. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and distribution of Notopappus pectinatus.
